data.title: Anthony Torres joins Pyramid Global Hospitality to lead award-winning culinary experiences. Celebrating recognitions from MICHELIN and Wine Spectator.
data.description: Pyramid Global Hospitality, a top hospitality management firm, is proud to announce its recent accolades in the food and beverage sector. This renowned company has been honored by MICHELIN and Wine Spectator for their exceptional offerings in Boston and The Woodlands, Texas. The recognitions highlight the company's dedication to providing top-notch culinary experiences. As of July 25, 2025, Pyramid Global Hospitality continues to uphold its commitment to excellence in the industry.

data.title: Top S&P 500 stock dubbed 'Best Name In AI Power' with strong earnings, leading U.S. AI progress.
data.description: The leading company in the S&P 500 index has emerged as the primary player driving the United States' artificial intelligence (AI) initiatives. Following its recent earnings report, this top-performing S&P 500 stock has been hailed as the premier enterprise in AI technology.

data.title: Piper Sandler gives Palantir an overweight rating with $170 price target, despite high valuation.
data.description: Palantir stock recently received an overweight rating from Piper Sandler with a price target of 170, despite its high valuation. The company's stock was well-received by investors and analysts.

data.title: Intel surpassed low Q2 revenue expectations, but revealed an unexpected loss. Intel stock plummeted.
data.description: In the second quarter, Intel surpassed revenue expectations despite facing challenges, yet unexpectedly reported a loss. Consequently, Intel's stock experienced a decline following this news.

data.title: Dow Jones index rises on economic data; Tesla stock up on robotaxi launch plans. Live coverage by Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Following unexpected positive economic data, the Dow Jones index experienced a surge. Additionally, Tesla shares saw a significant increase as the company announced its upcoming launch of a robotaxi service. The stock market showcased promising movement for the day, with the Dow continuing its gains and Tesla climbing due to the exciting development.

data.title: Dow Inc. (NYSE: DOW) reports Q2 loss, revenue down by 7% to $10.104 billion. Dividend cut by 50%. Analysts adjust forecasts.
data.description: Dow Inc. (NYSE: DOW) announced disappointing second-quarter financial results and reduced its dividend by 50% on Thursday. The company's adjusted loss per share of 42 cents was significantly higher than the expected 12 cents as per Wall Street's consensus estimates. Revenue dropped by 7% to $10.104 billion, falling short of the projected $10.252 billion. Dow reported a GAAP net loss of $801 million for the quarter, while its operating EBIT shifted to a loss of $21 million from $819 million in the same period last year. In response to these challenges, the company implemented several aggressive measures during the quarter. Detailed information can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nasdaq reports short interest positions in its stocks as of July 15, 2025: 3,260 securities with 13,792,841,090 shares, down from 14,138,758,851 shares in June.
data.description: In New York on July 25, 2025, it was reported that as of July 15, 2025, there was a short interest in 3,260 Nasdaq Global Market SM securities totaling 13,792,841,090 shares. This shows a decrease from the previous settlement date on June 30, 2025, where there were 14,138,758,851 shares in 3,257 Global Market issues. The short interest in mid-July represented 2.37 days, a decrease from the 2.59 days reported for the prior period.

data.title: NCMEC and Adobe collaborate at Comic-Con to reconstruct the face of San Diego John Doe, inviting public help.
data.description: This week at Comic-Con 2025 in San Diego, the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC) and Adobe have joined forces to showcase one of NCMEC's most perplexing unidentified child cases. Running from July 24 to July 27, this collaboration aims to raise awareness and engage the public in efforts to solve this mystery.

data.title: Carter's (NYSE: CRI) shares drop over tariff concerns, Q2 earnings miss estimate at 17 cents/share on $585.31 million sales (4% YoY). CEO cites pricing investments as cause.
data.description: Shares of Carter's, Inc. (NYSE: CRI) fell on Friday following disappointing earnings results and concerns over increasing tariffs. The company reported adjusted second-quarter earnings per share of 17 cents, falling short of the expected 34 cents. Despite this, quarterly sales reached $585.31 million, a 4% increase compared to the previous year and surpassing the Street's estimate of $561.14 million. Adjusted operating income for the quarter dropped to $11.8 million, down from $39.5 million in the same period last year. Carter's CEO & president, Douglas C. Palladini, expressed disappointment over the decrease in profitability, citing investments in pricing, new stores, and performance-based adjustments as contributing factors.

data.title: IMAX beats Q2 earnings with $0.26 per share, misses sales with $91.68 million. Analysts raise forecasts.
data.description: IMAX Corporation (NYSE: IMAX) released its second-quarter earnings results, showing a mix of strong and weak performance. The company exceeded analyst expectations for earnings, reporting 26 cents per share compared to the estimated 21 cents per share. However, IMAX fell short on quarterly sales, reporting $91.68 million versus the anticipated $92.51 million. CEO Rich Gelfond commented on the results, highlighting the company's success in various aspects such as network expansion globally, record-breaking box office numbers in North America, and gaining market share due to an increase in films utilizing IMAX technology. IMAX's financial performance in the second quarter indicated a positive momentum in key areas of its business.

data.title: OneFootball launches community sale for OneFootball Credits ($OFC) on CoinList, partnering with major brands.
data.description: Berlin, 25th July 2025 - OneFootball is excited to disclose the launch of the official community sale for OneFootball Credits ($OFC). Following the significant initial triumph of OneFootball Club, which introduced key collaborations with FIFA Rivals, Base (by Coinbase), Animoca Brands, Privy, and more, the sale of OFC tokens is now underway.
